rulings.Case Title: LOUISE DOUGLAS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 19, 2016, Line 1. RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. This writ arises out of July 17, 2015 Decision on Jurisdiction ("Decision"). The issues ripe for review are contained in the third paragraph of the Decision, specifically, (1) whether the ALJ erred in finding that the Rent Board does not have jurisdiction to determine the legality of the January 31, 2014 master lease; and (2) whether the ALJ erred in finding that the "undisputed facts show that the MOHCD continued to regulate the rents in conjunction with Mercy Housing after the prior master lease expired on January 31, 2014 whether or not it did so pursuant to a valid written lease or any lease at all." The Petition on pages 1-5 and/or pages 1-18 does not state sufficient facts to state a claim on either of these issues. Both sides appear to invite the Court to make a determination regarding the validity of the master lease on this petition (Motion 8:4-7; Opposition 2:6-9). The issue of validity of the master lease is not properly before the Court on this Petition. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Fix Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 19, 2016, Line 2. PETITIONER DANIEL SHAPERO Notice Of Motion And Motion To Fix Attorneys Fees is GRANTED as follows: $750 included in the memorandum of costs, plus $600 requested in this motion. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BONNIE KAY SPINDLER ET AL VS. ALISON WRIGHT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 19, 2016, Line 3. DEFENDANT ALISON WRIGHT DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer. Notices are sufficient and proper under Ellis Act and SFRO. =(501/REQ)
